seo.title: Claude Tool Use Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for Claude Tool Use agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams leveraging Claude Tool Use for tasks like case research, document review, and eDiscovery face heightened risks due to the potential for AI hallucinations, such as fabricated case citations, which have led to real-world sanctions as in Mata v. Avianca. Without robust compliance guardrails, tool use chains can cascade errors, parallel calls yield conflicting legal interpretations, and agentic actions might breach attorney-client privilege. Vex provides verifiable AI agent reliability to ensure outputs align with bar ethics, court standards, and privilege protections.

complianceRequirements.description: All AI-processed data must prevent leakage of confidential client information and ensure no external transmission without explicit consent.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies tool use chains to block privilege-violating actions and confirms no data exfiltration in agentic flows.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal advice or filings must be human-reviewed by licensed attorneys and cannot mislead courts or clients.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex flags inconsistent reasoning in extended thinking and ensures tool outputs match final legal recommendations.

complianceRequirements.description: Citations must reference real, verifiable cases; hallucinated precedents result in sanctions or dismissal.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex cross-verifies citations against legal databases during tool calls, rejecting fabricated references.

complianceRequirements.description: AI tools in discovery must preserve chain of custody, avoid tampering, and comply with FRCP 26(g).
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits parallel tool calls for conflicts and logs all screen interactions to maintain evidentiary integrity.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: Claude generates fake precedents like 'Varghese v. China Southern Airlines' leading to court sanctions.
industryRisks.example: Mata v. Avianca: Judge sanctioned lawyers for AI-fabricated citations undetected in tool use output.

industryRisks.name: Cascading tool use errors
industryRisks.description: Early tool failure in case search propagates false negatives to drafting, causing malpractice.
industryRisks.example: Failed database query leads to omitted key precedents in motion filing.

industryRisks.name: Privilege breach via agentic actions
industryRisks.description: Computer use agent screenshots or copies confidential docs without oversight.
industryRisks.example: Unintended screen scrape uploads client emails to unsecured tool endpoint.

industryRisks.name: Conflicting parallel tool results
industryRisks.description: Multiple research tools return divergent statute interpretations without resolution.
industryRisks.example: One tool claims statute of limitations expired; another does not, leading to erroneous advice.

implementation.title: Wrap Claude Tool Use with Vex Verify (Python)
implementation.description: Integrate Vex to verify Anthropic messages.create() tool responses against legal citation accuracy and privilege safeguards.
implementation.code:
import anthropic
import vex

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-key")

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    max_tokens=1024,
    tools=[{"name": "case_search", "input_schema": {...}}],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Research Mata v. Avianca citations"}]
)

verified = vex_client.verify(
    agent_run=message,
    policy="legal-citation-check",
    checks=["no_hallucinations", "privilege_safe"]
)
if verified:
    print(message.content[0].text)
else:
    raise ValueError("Legal compliance failure")

implementation.title: TypeScript Verification for Parallel Tool Calls
implementation.description: Use Vex SDK to resolve conflicts in parallel Claude tool calls for eDiscovery document review.

implementation.title: Audit Computer Use Agent for Screen Safety (Python)
implementation.description: Verify Claude computer use agent avoids unintended actions on legal docs or client portals.
implementation.code:
import anthropic
import vex

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-key")

response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    max_tokens=1024,
    tools=[{"name": "computer_use", "input_schema": {...}}],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Navigate PACER for case docs"}]
)

audit = vex_client.verify(
    agent_run=response,
    policy="legal-computer-use",
    checks=["no_unintended_clicks", "privilege_boundary"],
    context={"screen_elements": ["confidential", "public_docket"]}
)

if not audit.passed:
    print("Blocked risky action:", audit.issues)

caseStudyScenario.heading: Claude Tool Use in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized law firm deploys Claude Tool Use to accelerate eDiscovery in a class-action lawsuit involving thousands of documents. The agentic workflow chains case research tools with privilege scanners and parallel statute lookups, then uses computer use to navigate court dockets like PACER. Junior associates rely on the AI for initial drafts of motions, including citations pulled from tool outputs. Without guardrails, an early tool failure hallucinates a non-existent precedent, cascades to conflicting parallel results on liability statutes, and the computer agent inadvertently screenshots a privileged email during docket navigation.

The motion is filed with the fabricated citation, echoing the Mata v. Avianca debacle, triggering opposing counsel's motion for sanctions. The firm faces bar investigation for ethics violations and potential malpractice claims as client data exposure risks emerge from unverified agent actions.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex integrated via vex.verify() on messages.create(), the tool chain is halted at the hallucinated citation, flagged by the legal-citation-check policy. Parallel tool conflicts are resolved with verifiable consensus, and computer use audits block the privilege screenshot, logging chain of custody. The verified output prompts attorney review, averting sanctions. The firm processes eDiscovery 3x faster while maintaining compliance, avoiding millions in potential liability.
